mission.title: vault: Vault OIDC flow breaks fails to prompt users to login when their client tokens have expired
mission.description: Vault OIDC flow breaks fails to prompt users to login when their client tokens have expired. This issue affects 14+ users.

steps.title: Identify vault troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your vault installation:
```bash
vault version
vault status
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review vault configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vault configuration:
In older versions of vault a user would be prompted to log back into Vault when their Web UI client token had expired.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Vault OIDC flow breaks fails to prompt users to login when…
steps.description:
When the error handling changed in this PR: https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/pull/25953 the error handling failed to redirect users to authenticate in the OIDC provider workflow. 

> ### Testing
> I spent a while trying to figure out a way to revoke the user's token to simulate the reported bug
```yaml
# start the two versions of vault
[~]$ docker compose up

# configure terraform
[~]$    terraform init \
; terraform workspace new broken \
; terraform workspace new working

# bootstrap the older version of vault that wasn't busted (v1.15.6)
[~]$ terraform workspace select working

[~]$ terraform apply -var=vault_addr=http://localhost:8200

# bootstrap a more modern version where vault appears busted
[~]$ terraform workspace select broken

[~]$ terraform apply -var=vault_addr=http://localhost:8201
```

steps.title: Confirm Vault OIDC flow breaks fails to prompt users to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test vault to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
When the error handling changed in this PR: https://github.com/hashicorp/vault/pull/25953 the error handling failed to redirect users to authenticate in the OIDC provider workflow. 

> ### Testing
> I spent a while trying to figure out a way to revoke the user's token to simulate the reported bug but with our test  architecture that's very challenging.
codeSnippets:
# start the two versions of vault
[~]$ docker compose up

# configure terraform
[~]$    terraform init \
; terraform workspace new broken \
; terraform workspace new working

# bootstrap the older version of vault that wasn't busted (v1.15.6)
[~]$ terraform workspace select working

[~]$ terraform apply -var=vault_addr=http://localhost:8200

# bootstrap a more modern version where vault appears busted
[~]$ terraform workspace select broken

[~]$ terraform apply -var=vault_addr=http://localhost:8201

[~]$ vault token revoke \
  -address=http://localhost:8200 \
    hvs....

[~]$ vault token revoke \
  -address=http://localhost:8201 \
    hvs....

prerequisites.description: A working vault installation or development environment.
